name: Chang'e 1
fact: China's first lunar mission. Built and operated as a deliberate precursor test for the Chang'e 3 lander — the orbital phase served as a flight-qualification rehearsal for the deep-space communications and orbital mechanics that the lander mission would depend on. Crashed into the Moon at end of life as a controlled impact-test.
capability: First Chinese lunar mission; opened the Chang'e programme that delivered the only foreign sample-return missions (Chang'e 5, 6) of the 21st century.
